the normal English sound /g/ as in “go”
the normal English sound /h/ as in “hi.”
the /zh/ sound represented by the „s‟ in “pleasure”
This is not the normal English “r,” but a “flap” similar to the Spanish “r” or to the sound Americans make when they quickly say “gotta” as in “I gotta go.”
the “ee you” as in “see you later”
the definite article is made by adding the letter "L"
When you see an apostrophe, it indicates a "glottal stop"
Sometimes you will see syllables or even whole words without any vowels written in them. This is normal in TashlHeet.

english: He didn't go to the souq
example: He didn't go to the souq. ur iddi s s-suq.
She saw her friend. tzra tamddakultns.
She didn't see her friend. ur tzri tamddakultns.
They didn't eat couscous. ur ššin sksu.
I know/knew nothing. ur ssnġ walu.
I ate nothing. ur ššiġ walu.

He saw no one. ur izri ntta yan.
I have nothing. ur dari ntta yat tġawsa.
is jju tššit l-pitza? إِش جّى رػٍذ اٌـ ﭙرسح؟

english: He has never said these things
example: ur jju inni ġayad. أُور جّى إًِّٔ ؼٍبد .

example: I came (here) to Morocco last month. uškiġd s l-mġrib ayyur lli izrin.
